rulings.Case Title: FB GLOBAL PROPERTIES, A GENERAL PARTNERSHIP VS. CHRIS DIEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Application For Order For Sale Of A Dwelling And Issuance Of Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Real Property Should Not Be Made
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF FB GLOBAL PROPERTIES, A GENERAL PARTNERSHIP Application For Order For Sale Of A Dwelling And Issuance Of Order To Show Cause Why Order For Sale Of Real Property Should Not Be Made is GRANTED. The Order to Show Cause re: Sale of Dwelling shall issue. No opposition filed. Moving party to prepare an order. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HTLV SF LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. Demurer to the cause of action for Claim for Refund of Transfer Taxes- Statewide Concern is sustained with limited leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts (not conclusions) supporting their claim that the Transfer Tax (1) prohibited under the California Constitution (as long as this cause of action will not be duplicative of the 6th and 7th causes of action), (2) it imposes tax on transactions occurring outside the jurisdiction, (3) it imposes tax on the full market value of the property instead of the net value contemplated by Revenue and Taxation Code sections 11901, et seq., and (4) it contains non-conforming provisions not authorized nor contemplated by the Legislature. To the extent this cause of action is based on Documentary Transfer Tax Act, no leave to amend is granted. See CIM Urban REIT 211 Main St. (SF), LP v. City and County of San Francisco (2022) 75 Cal.App.5th 939 Demurrer to the causes of action for Claim for Refund of Transfer Taxes- Cal Const XIII A &C, Prop. W Component and Claim for Refund of Transfer Taxes- Cal Const XIII A &C, Prop. I Component is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. See Johnson v. County of Mendocino (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 1017. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALLEN COMMUNITY HOUSING INITIATIVE LLC VS. TIFFANY RUGLEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 2, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT TIFFANY RUGLEY, ISYSS LYONS DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. While Defendant argues in the demurrer that the Complaint alleges the premises to be HUD-regulated, Defendant points to no such allegation in the complaint. New argument regarding compliance with SFRO requirements raised for the first time in the reply will not be considered by the Court as it is not timely raised and Plaintiff was not provided sufficient notice and opportunity to respond. The Court does not reach the merits of this argument at this time. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
